title: What Being Hard on Yourself Actually Means for a Man of God
caption:
Somewhere along the way, "hard on yourself" got confused with self-hatred. They're not the same thing.

A man of God is ruthless with his own sin and slow to anger with everyone else's. He judges the log in his own eye before he mentions the speck in his brother's. He confesses before he corrects. He gives grace generously, except to himself.

Jesus wept at Lazarus's grave before he ever raised him. He grieved before he acted. A brother who's failing needs that same order from you, tears before verdict.

"Why do you see the speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not notice the log that is in your own eye?" — Matthew 7:3

Hard on your sin. Never hard on yourself.

#faith #grace #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #scripture #christianity
theme: He's hard on himself, gentle with others.
